legal_background.problem: No specific legal right to housing exists in Australian law or the Constitution.
legal_background.current_state: Victoria, ACT, and Queensland have Human Rights Acts but none explicitly protect housing. South Australia has no Human Rights Act.
legal_background.recent_win: ACT passed legislation in 2025 enshrining housing as a foundational social good with legal challenge mechanisms.
legal_background.federal_deadline: Australian government must respond to UN housing rights recommendations by July 2026.
legal_background.goal: Pressure South Australia and the federal government to add housing to a Human Rights Act.

character.name: Renter
character.concept: A relatable teenager navigating an absurd housing market. Not a superhero or politician — just a kid doing the maths and getting increasingly bewildered by what they find.
character.tone: Frustrated but funny. Earnest. Never preachy.
character.visual_style: Simple flat illustration, bold outlines, limited colour palette matching brand
character.reference: Kurzgesagt bird characters — character IS the brand, creator stays anonymous

hook.viral_question: Should a roof over your head be a human right — yes or no?
hook.rationale: Binary question forces a side. Drives shares and comments automatically.
hook.villain_frame: Politicians who own multiple investment properties get to vote on housing policy.
hook.key_stat: An Australian teenager today would need to save every dollar they earn for 12+ years just to afford a deposit — before rent, food, or living.

content_series.name: The Maths
content_series.concept: Brutal, simple arithmetic. One stat. One character reaction. One angry emoji.
examples: Average Adelaide house price ÷ average teen part-time wage = 47 years of saving
Australian politicians own an average of X investment properties. You own 0.
The ACT just made housing a legal right. SA hasn't. Here's the difference.

content_series.name: Renter Explains
content_series.concept: Character walks through one housing concept per episode. Script-driven. No voiceover required — text on screen or AI voice.
examples: Why do politicians own so many investment properties?
What is negative gearing and why should you care?
What happened in the ACT that could change everything
I tried to take the government to court over housing. Here's what I found.
Why your generation will never own homes — and what we're doing about it

content_series.name: Real Stories
content_series.concept: Crowdsourced stories from other teenagers about their housing fears. Collected via DM or form. Turned into illustrated cards. No faces, no names required.

platforms.name: TikTok
platforms.algorithm_tip: Post 3x/day for first 2 weeks. Consistency beats quality early.

platforms.name: Instagram
platforms.primary_use: Reach older teens and parents via Reels reposts

platforms.name: YouTube
platforms.primary_use: Long-form explainers, press pickup, teacher sharing

platforms.name: Discord
platforms.primary_use: Organising teens who want to act — petition drives, school poster drops, story sharing

platforms.name: Change.org
platforms.primary_use: Petition with climbing signature count used as content itself
platforms.ask: Add housing to South Australia's Human Rights Act

platforms.name: LinkedIn
platforms.primary_use: Reach journalists, politicians, lawyers, housing policy advocates
platforms.note: A 16-year-old leading a housing rights campaign is inherently newsworthy to this audience

amplification_strategy.biggest_lever: One larger Australian TikTok creator duetting a video
amplification_strategy.target_creator_profile: Australian finance-for-young-people, cost-of-living, or youth politics creators with 50k–500k followers
amplification_strategy.outreach_approach: Pitch a duet or stitch. Don't ask for a share — give them content to react to.

do: Lead with identity and injustice, not policy
Give people a specific action (sign, share, comment your MP's name)
Use lo-fi phone-camera aesthetic for authenticity
Post character reaction to real news as it happens
Use the July 2026 UN deadline as a live countdown
dont: Ask people to 'raise awareness' without a specific action
Use over-produced graphics — raw beats polished for this audience
Post on Twitter/X — negligible Australian teen audience

week_1.detail: Sketch Renter in 3 poses: neutral, shocked, angry
Canva account, colour palette, campaign handle registration on TikTok + Instagram
Single brutal stat infographic with Renter reaction
Post to TikTok (with CapCut motion) + Instagram. Pin to profile.
Set up petition: 'Add housing to SA Human Rights Act'. Target: 1,000 signatures.
Write script for Episode 1: 'Why your generation will never own a home'
Check analytics, refine based on what resonated

phase_1_30_days.goal: Proof of concept — show momentum exists

phase_3_july_2026.goal: Submission to federal government UN response window

animation_starter: PowerPoint — slide elements to fake motion

political_window.event: Australian government must formally respond to UN Universal Periodic Review recommendations on housing rights
political_window.opportunity: A teen-led campaign with measurable momentum landing before this deadline has direct political leverage
political_window.reviewers: 120+ countries called on Australia to improve housing rights at the January 2026 UN Human Rights Council review
